summary: Copilot Cowork reached GA on June 16 with new pricing model and capabilities impacting Day 2 materials; Copilot Studio Unified Canvas and Computer-Using Agents GA impact Day 3; M365 Copilot Claude model integration and licensing changes affect Day 1 Session 2.

materials.title: Manager Kickoff Presentation
materials.finding: Microsoft messaging has shifted from AI-first assistants to agent-first autonomous workflows (Build 2026, Copilot Cowork GA). Deck may reference outdated framing if it positions Copilot as an assistant rather than an autonomous agent platform.
materials.suggestedAction: Review slides referencing AI assistants and update to reflect agent-first positioning, autonomous delegation via Cowork, and usage-based billing model.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Guide talking points may reference Copilot as a per-seat productivity tool. Cowork GA introduces usage-based billing (Copilot Credits) which changes the cost/value discussion managers need to have with teams.
materials.suggestedAction: Update cost/value framing to mention Copilot Credits model and agent-first delegation capabilities that managers should set expectations around.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video talk track likely references pre-GA Copilot capabilities. With Cowork GA and agent-first positioning, the program value proposition presented to managers has evolved.
materials.suggestedAction: Review video script for outdated positioning; consider re-recording segments that describe program outcomes if they do not mention autonomous agent capabilities.

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: Microsoft now positions its AI strategy as agent-first rather than AI-first assistants. Build 2026 introduced Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0, Microsoft IQ intelligence layer, and seven new MAI models. If deck frames AI-first mindset purely around chat-based assistance, it may not reflect the current autonomous-agent paradigm.
materials.suggestedAction: Review foundational framing slides. Consider incorporating agent-first language, autonomous delegation concepts, and Microsoft IQ as the intelligence backbone connecting enterprise AI.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator talking points around AI-first operating model should incorporate the shift from prompt-response AI to autonomous agent workflows that Microsoft announced at Build 2026.
materials.suggestedAction: Update key talking points to reference agent-first paradigm and CI-before-AI philosophy Microsoft is promoting internally.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video establishes foundational mindset concepts. Core message about embracing AI in workflows remains valid; specific product references are minimal in icebreaker content.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 1 — M365 Copilot: From Overwhelmed to In-Command

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: Multiple breaking changes since deck was created: (1) Claude model now available in Copilot Chat as of June 16 release, (2) Copilot in Word/Excel/PowerPoint/OneNote now requires M365 Copilot license—unlicensed users get limited chat-only access, (3) new unified visual design in Edge Copilot, (4) infinite scroll chat history replaces paginated view. Screenshots and feature descriptions likely outdated.
materials.suggestedAction: Update screenshots showing Copilot Chat UI (model picker with Claude option, infinite scroll). Update any slides about feature availability to reflect new licensing requirements. Add mention of multi-model choice capability.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Guide likely references single-model Copilot Chat experience. Claude is now selectable in Copilot Chat, licensing model changed (in-app Copilot requires M365 Copilot license), and new features like infinite scroll history are available. Facilitator may give inaccurate information about access and capabilities.
materials.suggestedAction: Update talking points about model selection (Claude now available), licensing requirements for in-app vs chat-only access, and new UX patterns (infinite scroll). Add facilitator note about demonstrating model picker.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video walkthrough of M365 Copilot will show pre-June 16 UI without Claude model option, old chat history pagination, and pre-redesign Edge visual. Demonstrations will not match what participants see in their own environment.
materials.suggestedAction: Re-record video segments showing Copilot Chat interface, including model picker with Claude, new infinite scroll, and updated Edge design. Or add facilitator disclaimer noting UI differences.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: Lab exercises using Copilot Chat may not account for the new model picker (Claude option) or changed licensing for in-app Copilot features. Participants may see different UI than described in lab steps.
materials.suggestedAction: Verify lab step screenshots match current Copilot Chat UI. Add optional stretch exercise letting participants try Claude model for comparison. Confirm all lab steps work with current licensing.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 2 — Copilot Cowork: Delegate, Don’t Dictate

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: Copilot Cowork reached General Availability on June 16, 2026. Major changes: (1) usage-based billing via Copilot Credits replaces any preview pricing, (2) new Model Picker with Claude Opus/Sonnet and GPT-5.5, (3) browser automation capability, (4) integrated image generation via Imagen 2, (5) expanded plugin catalog including Dynamics 365/Jira/Salesforce/SAP. Deck created during preview will show outdated UI, missing features, and incorrect availability information.
materials.suggestedAction: Major deck revision needed: update all screenshots to GA interface, add slides on Copilot Credits billing model, model picker, browser automation, and expanded plugin ecosystem. Remove any preview/beta disclaimers.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator guide references Cowork in preview state. GA launch changed: availability (now all tenants globally), pricing (Copilot Credits usage-based), admin controls (off by default, granular group policies), and capabilities (browser automation, image generation, multi-model). Facilitator will deliver inaccurate information about access, pricing, and feature scope.
materials.suggestedAction: Rewrite availability and pricing sections. Update facilitator notes on admin prerequisites (Cowork must be enabled by admin). Add talking points for new capabilities: browser automation, model selection, Copilot Credits cost management.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video demonstrates Cowork in preview state. GA interface includes model picker, browser automation panel, image generation, and new plugin marketplace UI. Video demonstrations will not match participant experience and may reference limitations that no longer exist.
materials.suggestedAction: Re-record video using GA Cowork interface. Demonstrate model picker, browser automation, and at least one third-party plugin. Update talk track to reference Copilot Credits billing.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: Lab exercises for Cowork were designed for preview. GA changes: (1) admin must explicitly enable Cowork (off by default), (2) UI now includes model picker and browser automation, (3) billing via Copilot Credits means lab exercises consume measurable credits. Lab steps, screenshots, and prerequisites are likely all affected.
materials.suggestedAction: Rewrite lab prerequisites to include admin enablement verification. Update all step-by-step instructions and screenshots to GA interface. Add cost awareness note about Copilot Credits consumption during labs. Consider adding exercises for browser automation and model selection.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 3 — Build Your Own Copilot: No Code Required

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: Copilot Studio underwent major changes in June 2026: (1) Computer-Using Agents reached GA allowing agents to interact with application UIs directly, (2) new Unified Workflows Canvas replaces previous builder interface with drag-and-drop agent-first design, (3) multi-agent workflows with A2A protocol in preview, (4) generative actions allow dynamic plugin selection. Deck likely shows old authoring canvas and does not cover CUA or multi-agent capabilities.
materials.suggestedAction: Major revision needed: update all builder interface screenshots to Unified Canvas, add slides on Computer-Using Agents capability, update architecture diagrams to show multi-agent coordination, and refresh the no-code value proposition to include generative actions.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Guide instructions reference the pre-Unified Canvas Copilot Studio interface. The authoring experience has been fundamentally redesigned with drag-and-drop agent-first workflow design. Step-by-step facilitator instructions will not match current UI. Additionally, Computer-Using Agents and generative actions expand what no-code means significantly.
materials.suggestedAction: Rewrite builder walkthrough sections to match Unified Canvas. Update facilitator demo scripts. Add talking points on CUA capabilities and how generative actions reduce manual topic creation. Update prerequisites/setup instructions.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video demonstrates Copilot Studio with pre-Unified Canvas interface. The entire authoring UI has been redesigned. Video walkthroughs will show a builder experience that no longer exists, making it confusing for facilitators preparing to demonstrate live.
materials.suggestedAction: Re-record video using the new Unified Canvas interface. Include demonstration of Computer-Using Agents setup and generative actions. Update talk track to reflect agent-first workflow design paradigm.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: Lab exercises guide participants through building agents in Copilot Studio. The Unified Canvas redesign means all step-by-step UI instructions are outdated. Navigation paths, button locations, and workflow for creating agents have fundamentally changed.
materials.suggestedAction: Complete rewrite of lab steps required to match Unified Canvas. Consider adding exercises for Computer-Using Agents (no-API automation) and generative actions. Update all screenshots and navigation instructions.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 4 — Work Redesign: Rethinking the Work Itself

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: Microsoft now promotes CI-before-AI philosophy (optimize workflows first, then automate) and agent-first work redesign. If deck frames work redesign only around augmenting existing tasks with AI chat, it misses the autonomous agent delegation model that Cowork GA enables.
materials.suggestedAction: Review work redesign framework slides. Consider incorporating CI-before-AI philosophy and examples of full workflow delegation via Cowork rather than just task augmentation.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Work redesign discussion may not account for agent-first delegation capabilities now GA. Facilitator should be able to reference concrete Cowork examples where entire workflows are delegated, not just individual tasks augmented.
materials.suggestedAction: Add facilitator talking points with real-world Cowork delegation examples. Reference CI-before-AI approach for process optimization before automation.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Video covers conceptual work redesign frameworks. Core principles of rethinking work (not just automating it) remain aligned with Microsoft current messaging. No product-specific demonstrations that would be outdated.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: Lab exercises for work redesign may benefit from incorporating Cowork delegation scenarios now that it is GA. Current exercises may focus solely on prompt-based augmentation rather than full workflow delegation.
materials.suggestedAction: Consider adding an optional Cowork-based exercise where participants delegate an end-to-end workflow rather than just augmenting individual steps.
